CheckTitle: AI Agent Training Data Sources Must Have Integrity Verification
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Data Access**. Confirms that all training data sources used by the agent are validated with cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., checksums, digital signatures) before training. This prevents data poisoning attacks that can introduce backdoors or biases into the model.
Risk: Unverified training data can be poisoned by adversaries to embed backdoors, manipulate model behaviour, or introduce systematic biases. Data poisoning attacks are difficult to detect post-training.

Code.Other:
1. Compute and store checksums (SHA-256) for all training datasets at source.
2. Verify checksums before each training run and fail the pipeline on mismatch.
3. Use signed dataset manifests where possible to detect tampering.

Recommendation.Text: Implement cryptographic integrity verification for all training data sources. Automate verification in the training pipeline and alert on failures.

Notes: ASPM-050 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
